commit.message:
fix: resolve four document-implementation inconsistencies

1. eval_gate.py — implement strategy field (was documented but ignored)
   - "all" (default): all optimize metrics must improve
   - "primary": only first optimize metric must improve
   - "pareto": at least one improves, none regress below baseline
   - strategy now included in output JSON

2. lint_contract.py — enforce Stop rules for threshold/continuous loops
   - Added "Stop rules" to RECOMMENDED_FIELDS (warning when absent)
   - If Loop mode contains threshold/continuous/high-autonomy,
     Stop rules becomes a hard error instead of a warning

3. schemas.md — add split and tags fields to trigger-eval-set.json docs
   - Actual file has split and tags; docs only had query/should_trigger/note

4. README/README.ko.md — fix script count (21 → 19 scripts + 3 graders)

commit.message:
Add SimplePaint example, Path type, unified Canvas API, WindowResizability

SimplePaint: MacPaint-class drawing app with pencil, eraser, line,
rectangle, ellipse tools, color palette, brush size, undo/redo.
Cross-platform: macOS (real SwiftUI), GTK4, Win32.

Core:
- Path type with move/line/rect/ellipse/arc/curve elements
- Layout-sized Canvas init with CGSize
- Shading and StrokeStyle types for Canvas drawing
- WindowResizability API (.contentSize, .contentMinSize, .automatic)

GTK4:
- Canvas narrow-mutation path (in-place redraw without widget teardown)
- Path-based stroke/fill on DrawingContext
- WindowResizability wired to gtk_window_set_resizable
- Divider orientation fix in HStack
- Slider rebuild deferral removed (was blocking rebuilds)
- Button, TapGestureView, DragGestureView, ScrollView now GTKDescribable

Win32:
- Path-based stroke/fill via ID2D1PathGeometry
- LineCap and lineJoin via D2D stroke styles
- Canvas live redraw on @State change
- WindowResizability wired to WS_THICKFRAME
- Flat buttons, emoji font, border width fixes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: WSL2 LevelDB LOCK根本修正 — userData を /dev/shm (tmpfs) に移動

WSL2のext4ファイルシステムでElectron/Chromium内部のfcntlロックが
正常動作しない問題の根本修正。

- /dev/shm（RAM-backed tmpfs）はPOSIX完全準拠のファイルロックをサポート
- --in-process-gpu でプロセス数削減（ロック競合リスク低減）
- 注意: /dev/shmはRAM上のためWSL再起動でデータ消失（再ログイン必要）

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: three performance-neutral GPU efficiency improvements

IMPROVEMENT 1 — Idle clock management:
  GPU util < 5% for 3 ticks → apply minimum supported clocks.
  GPU util rises above 15% → instant restore via nvidia-smi -rac.
  Reduces idle draw from ~20W toward hardware minimum.

IMPROVEMENT 2 — Memory-bound workload detection:
  SM util < 40% AND mem util > 80% for 5 ticks → reduce core
  clock to 75% of max (memory clock stays at max).
  SM util rises above 60% → instant clock restore.
  No throughput impact on bandwidth-limited workloads.

IMPROVEMENT 3 — Thermal headroom tracking:
  Every tick logs thermal_headroom (spike_trigger - current_temp)
  and whether GPU boost is sustained at max clock. When headroom
  > 10°C and boost sustained, logs fan_optimization_benefit=confirmed
  as a measurable proof point that CooledAI fan optimization
  enables better GPU boost consistency.

All three improvements use utilization-based queries (nvidia-smi
--query-gpu=utilization.gpu,utilization.memory) rather than
temperature bands for fixed-TDP GPUs (T4). Efficiency gate still
applies — never reduces clocks when power >90W, near spike temp,
and power ramping.

219 tests pass (49 governor tests including idle, memory_bound,
compute_bound, and thermal_headroom test cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: make install.sh work non-interactively and repair Docker install test (#796)

* fix: make install.sh work non-interactively and repair Docker install test

Five issues fixed, all blocking non-interactive and Docker-based installs:

1. Docker install-test was trivially passing without running install.sh
   (issues #792). Replace the manual directory-creation sequence in
   docker-compose with run-install-test.sh, which actually invokes
   install.sh --non-interactive and then asserts on lobster CLI
   availability, runtime directories, and Python package imports.

2. Tarball mode was the default for fresh installs, pulling a stale
   v0.1.0 release tag (issue #787). Switch the default to git clone
   (always current from main). Tarball mode is no longer reachable from
   the default path; existing git installs continue to update in place.

3. Internet connectivity check hard-failed when source was already
   present (issue #791). Skip the check when LOBSTER_INSTALL_DIR already
   has install.sh (source pre-copied, as in Docker test environments).

4. Claude Code installation in non-interactive mode tried to curl-pipe
   the claude.ai installer (needed internet + interactive shell). Skip
   gracefully in --non-interactive mode with a hint to complete manually.

5. systemd service install (sudo cp + systemctl daemon-reload) ran
   unconditionally, failing in Docker containers where systemd is absent.
   Guard with a pidof/systemctl is-system-running check; skip cleanly
   with an info message in non-systemd environments.

Two smaller fixes:
- Pre-commit hook cp no longer crashes when .git is absent (now guarded)
- Docker group "WARN" on fresh machines with no Docker is now "INFO"
  (issue #786); WARN is reserved for the case where Docker is installed
  but the group is missing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: align internet-check skip with git-clone skip and guard gh auth prompt

Two fixes for headless/CI install gaps:

1. Internet check now skips when NON_INTERACTIVE=true and source is already
   present (matching the git-clone skip condition at line 897). Previously
   it only skipped for .git dirs or --dev mode, so a Docker container with
   pre-copied source and no .git would hit the internet check unnecessarily.

2. GitHub CLI auth prompt now skips in non-interactive mode. Previously the
   read -p prompt had no NON_INTERACTIVE guard — a headless container with no
   gh auth session and no GITHUB_PAT would hang waiting for input (the
   || true only suppressed exit, not the hang).

Docker install-test passes: exit 0, all assertions green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
init-nnue: redesign weight initialization for TDLeaf training

FT weights N(0,5) — acc std ≈ √30×5 ≈ 27, SqrCReLU sweet spot.
FC weights N(0,{1,3,2}) — small to keep initial positional ≈ 0 cp.
PSQT: pure material values (no piece-square bonuses), same all buckets.
All means zero (He/Kaiming principle, decoupled from SF15.1 statistics).

commit.message:
Fix wrong-answer flickering by removing layout-shifting shake animation

The QuizStep shake animation fired 5 rapid setShakeOffset state updates
over 200ms, each changing marginLeft and forcing Ink to re-layout the
entire component tree — causing visible flickering especially in narrow
terminals. Removed the multi-frame shake in favor of the existing red
COMPROMISED text feedback. Also replaced StatusBar's prevCover state
with a ref to eliminate an unnecessary render cycle on cover drops.

commit.message:
feat: add brag sync-cache command to push local cache to GitHub (EDU-7)

Adds the `brag sync-cache` subcommand that reads all JSON entries from
~/.brag/cache/ and uploads to the configured GitHub storage repo any
entries not already present. Deduplication uses SourceURL for synced
entries (github/jira) and ID for manual entries, with a single API call
to list existing entries up front for efficiency.

commit.message:
Fix potion-while-holding-bow, add item icons, remove badge overlap

Potion/seed/bucket are instant-use — no longer change activeHotbarSlot,
so pressing potion slot doesn't falsely highlight it while keeping the
bow out. Only gun/melee/hoe change the active highlight.

Added real pixel art icons for non-weapon quickslots:
- Potion: full bottle with fill level + count display + cooldown overlay
- Bucket: metal pail with handle and water line
- Seed: colored packet with seed dots
- Hoe: handle + angled head (same as farm hoe art)

Removed QS badge circle from gun/melee quickslots (was overlapping tier
labels). Badge only shows for non-weapon quickslots now.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add WGSL shader variants for batch, UI, and sprite batch render paths

The wgpu backend rejects bare `uniform` GLSL declarations that lack
`layout(binding=X)` annotations. Three rendering paths (batch sprite
renderer, UI render system, sprite batch asset loader) only had GLSL
shaders, causing silent failures on the wgpu backend.

This commit:
- Adds a `ShaderLanguage` enum (Glsl/Wgsl) to `BackendInfo` and a
  `shader_language()` default method on the `RenderBackend` trait
- Provides WGSL shader variants for all three broken paths using the
  same bind group layout as the working text and immediate shaders
- Migrates all existing ad-hoc backend detection (`info().name == "wgpu"`,
  `is_wgsl_backend()`) to the typed `match backend.shader_language()`
  pattern across immediate, text, 3D, SDK, and FFI render paths
- Makes `ShaderSource::validate()` language-aware so WGSL assets pass
  validation without requiring `#version` or `void main()`
- Adds safe_viewport div-by-zero guard to UI GLSL vertex shader
- Removes now-unused `is_wgsl_backend()` from NativeRenderBackend

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix 5 pre-launch issues: bashrc, reconnect, git hygiene, idle timeout, cache

1. Fix .bashrc Permission denied: exec now bootstraps /etc/skel to tmpfs
   home before spawning bash (entrypoint only runs on container start)
2. Fix dead terminal on reconnect: client clears stale tabs on Socket.IO
   reconnect so server's fresh exec renders cleanly
3. Git hygiene: .gitignore hashed bundles, remove tracked ones
4. Idle session timeout: sessions with no users auto-stop after 1 hour
   idle. 60-second sweep interval checks both idle and expired sessions.
5. Activity heartbeat: terminal input and chat messages touch session
   lastActivity to prevent premature idle timeout

repository.name: lciclops
repository.description: LC Visor de Datos

commit.message:
Fix mobile map zoom reset bug

On mobile, pinch-to-zoom on the Leaflet map was causing the map to snap
back to its previous position. This was caused by three interacting issues:

1. Missing viewport constraints: without maximum-scale=1.0 and
   user-scalable=no, the browser tried to zoom the page AND the map
   simultaneously during pinch gestures, causing viewport resize events
   that Leaflet interpreted as container resize.

2. Unstable container height: the .app container used 100vh which changes
   when the mobile address bar shows/hides during touch interactions.
   Switched to position:fixed + inset:0 which is immune to address bar
   resize. Also disabled the grid-template-columns transition on mobile
   since it's unused (grid is always 1fr) but could trigger Leaflet
   resize detection.

3. PostHog autocapture interference: the default autocapture config added
   event listeners to all DOM elements including the map canvas, which
   could interfere with Leaflet's touch handling. Restricted autocapture
   to only standard form/link elements.

4. Added touch-action:none on #map to prevent the browser from
   intercepting touch gestures meant for Leaflet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

2. Button selection: add :not(.vbtn-active/.sbtn-active/.abtn-active/.hbtn-active)

3. Performance: move all [style*=""] attribute selectors from dynamic
   JS-injected stylesheet to static <style> block — eliminates the
   useEffect that created/destroyed a 70-rule stylesheet on every toggle,
   triggering full page reflow

Auto-generate Appfile and copy Fastfile from action

Instead of requiring the caller to provide package_name, the Fastfile
now reads applicationId from build.gradle.kts and PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER
from the Xcode project to generate the Appfile automatically. The action
copies its own Fastfile into the project, removing the need for
import_from_git.

repository.name: flutter-deploy
repository.description: Build and deploy Flutter apps via Fastlane

commit.message:
fix: prepend sudo to nvidia-smi -pl for non-root agent execution

The cooledai agent runs as cooledaiadmin (non-root), but nvidia-smi -pl
requires elevated privileges. Added sudo prefix to the power limit
command in set_gpu_power_limit_w(). Paired with a sudoers rule at
/etc/sudoers.d/cooledai-nvidia on the pilot node granting passwordless
access to nvidia-smi -i and -pl flags only.

Confirmed: 0 apply_failed errors after deploy (previously 100% failure).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Switch debug logging to stderr for immediate output under Wine

Wine's CRT buffers stdout even with fflush. stderr is unbuffered
by default and shows output immediately.

Merge `kafka_settings` into `settings` (#107)

Consolidate all Kafka configuration from kafka_settings.py into the main
settings.py file to follow Django best practices and improve
maintainability.

Changes:
- Move all Kafka topic configurations, SSL settings, and KAFKA_SETTINGS
  dict from kafka_settings.py to settings.py
- Update all imports across the codebase to use django.conf.settings
  instead of project_settings.kafka_settings
- Update references from kafka_settings.* to settings.* in all modules
- Remove the now-unused kafka_settings.py file

This refactoring simplifies the configuration structure and ensures all
settings are accessible through the standard Django settings module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Signed-off-by: Paul Wayper <paulway@redhat.com>
